ccel.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

title: To John, Abbot.

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Gregory to John, Abbot of Mount Sina.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: The Epistle of thy Humility testifies to the holiness of thy life; whence we give great thanks to Almighty God, for that we know that there are still some to pray for our sins.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For we, under the colour of ecclesiastical government, are tossed in the billows of this world, which frequently overwhelm us.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But by the protecting hand of heavenly grace we are raised up again from the deep.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Do you, then, who lead a tranquil life in the so great serenity of your rest, and stand as it were safe on the shore, extend the hand of your prayer to us who are on our voyage, or rather who are suffering shipwreck, and with all the supplications in your power help us as we strive to reach the land of the living, so that not only for your own life, but also for our rescue, you may have reward for ever.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: May the Holy Trinity protect thy Love with the right hand of Its protection, and grant unto thee in Its sight, by praying, by admonishing, by shewing example of good work, to feed the flock committed to thee, that so thou mayest be able to reach the pastures of eternal life with the flock itself which thou feedest.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For it is written, My sheep shall come and shall find pastures (John x.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And these pastures in truth we find, when, freed from the winter of this life, we are satisfied with the greenness of eternal life, as of a new Spring.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: We have learnt from the report of our son Simplicius that there is a want of beds and bedding in the Gerontocomium, which has been constructed by one Isaurus there.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Wherefore we have sent 15 cloaks, 30 rachanæ, and 15 beds.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: We have also given money for the purchase of mattresses and for their transport, which we beg thy Love not to disdain, but to supply them to the place for which they have been sent.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Given on the day of the Kalends of September, Indiction 4.
